growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with lactate/pyruvate/acetate as substrate
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: Desulfovibrio sp. strains S3(T) and S3-i utilized structurally less complex carbon substrates, such as lactate, pyruvate, and acetate ... both strains exhibited relatively slow growth with doubling times of 2-3 days
growth_records.notes: Doubling time 2-3 days on simple substrates; midpoint 2.5 d = 60 h; mu = ln2/60 = 0.01155 h-1 (range: ln2/72=0.00963 to ln2/48=0.01444). Dissimilatory sulfate reducer and H2-consuming terminal oxidizer (genome-inferred, no measured uptake rate). Lactate concentration not specified numerically. Strain S3-i reported jointly with same td.

growth_records.strain: DMSS-1 (Cape Cod salt marsh isolate, Sim et al. 2011b)
growth_records.medium: Sim et al. 2011b medium (21 g/L NaCl, +Se) + lactate, PO4 360 uM

sec.method: Colorimetric assay; up to ~10.7 mM SumH2S

mfa.note: 2 lactate + SO4 2- -> 2 acetate + 2 HCO3- + HS- + H+

growth_records.cite: Zaarur S et al. Influence of Phosphorus and Cell Geometry on the Fractionation of Sulfur Isotopes by Several Species of Desulfovibrio during Microbial Sulfate Reduction. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.00890. [PMC5447228]
growth_records.snippet: Desulfovibrio sp. DMSS-1 Lactate 360 ... k up to 1.31 ... csSRR 379-824 ... SumH2S up to 10.7 mM.
growth_records.notes: Table 3, DMSS-1 at 360 uM PO4. Max k=1.31 day-1 -> mu=0.0546 h-1; td=ln2/1.31=0.529 day=12.7 h. csSRR 379-824 fmol/cell/day (small fast-dividing cells, ~2.6x0.7 um). Lactate concentration not restated (follows Sim et al. 2011b).

growth_records.strain: DMSS-1 (Cape Cod salt marsh isolate, Sim et al. 2011b)
growth_records.medium: Sim et al. 2011b medium + lactate, PO4 36 uM

up.method: Table 3 range 862-939 fmol/cell/day (highest csSRR in study)

sec.method: Colorimetric assay; up to ~12 mM SumH2S

growth_records.cite: Zaarur S et al. Influence of Phosphorus and Cell Geometry on the Fractionation of Sulfur Isotopes by Several Species of Desulfovibrio during Microbial Sulfate Reduction. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.00890. [PMC5447228]
growth_records.snippet: Desulfovibrio sp. DMSS-1 Lactate 36 ... k up to 1.91 ... csSRR 862-939 ... SumH2S up to 12 mM.
growth_records.notes: Table 3, DMSS-1 at 36 uM PO4. Max k=1.91 day-1 -> mu=0.0796 h-1; td=ln2/1.91=0.363 day=8.7 h (fastest growth in study). csSRR 862-939 fmol/cell/day (highest cell-specific sulfate reduction rate observed). Consistent with asymptotic 34-epsilon lower limit ~5 permil at csSRR >100 fmol/cell/day.

growth_records.strain: DMSS-1 (Cape Cod salt marsh isolate, Sim et al. 2011b)
growth_records.medium: Sim et al. 2011b medium + lactate, phosphate-limited (5 uM PO4)

sec.method: Colorimetric assay; up to ~11.7 mM SumH2S

growth_records.cite: Zaarur S et al. Influence of Phosphorus and Cell Geometry on the Fractionation of Sulfur Isotopes by Several Species of Desulfovibrio during Microbial Sulfate Reduction. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.00890. [PMC5447228]
growth_records.snippet: Desulfovibrio sp. DMSS-1 Lactate 5 ... k up to 0.21 ... csSRR 149-165 ... SumH2S up to 11.7 mM.
growth_records.notes: Table 3, DMSS-1 severe P-limitation (5 uM). Max k=0.21 day-1 -> mu=0.00875 h-1; td=ln2/0.21=3.30 day=79.2 h. csSRR 149-165 fmol/cell/day. At <1 uM PO4 DMSS-1 did not grow (k~0, csSRR~0) - not recorded as a separate growth record.
